problem:
The number
\[e^{7\pi i/60} + e^{17\pi i/60} + e^{27 \pi i/60} + e^{37\pi i /60} + e^{47 \pi i /60}\]is expressed in the form $r e^{i \theta}$, where $0 \le \theta < 2\pi$.  Find $\theta$.

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the geometric representation of $e^{7\pi i/60}$ in the complex plane?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What about the geometric representation of $e^{17\pi i/60}$? How does it differ from the first representation?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the geometric representation of $e^{27 \pi i/60}$? How is this different from the previous two?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Continuing on, what about the geometric representation of $e^{37\pi i /60}$? How is it related to the others?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Finally, how would you represent $e^{47 \pi i /60}$ in the complex plane? Is there a pattern in the geometric representations of all five numbers?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Given the geometric representations of all these numbers, can you intuitively determine the argument of the sum $e^{7\pi i/60} + e^{17\pi i/60} + e^{27 \pi i/60} + e^{37\pi i /60} + e^{47 \pi i /60}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use the idea of symmetry to simplify the problem of finding the argument of the sum?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the symmetry present in the complex plane representation of these numbers?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Using symmetry, what can we say about the argument of the sum of $e^{7\pi i/60}$ and $e^{47\pi i /60}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the argument of $e^{17\pi i/60} + e^{37\pi i/60}$ relate to the argument of $e^{7\pi i/60} + e^{47\pi i /60}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Combining the arguments from steps 9 and 10, can we find the argument of the entire sum of $e^{7\pi i/60} + e^{17\pi i/60} + e^{27 \pi i/60} + e^{37\pi i /60} + e^{47 \pi i /60}$?
classified_questions.label: b
